summary: Matched Pichu's fighter source file by replacing asm/melee/ft/chara/ftpichu.s with src/melee/ft/chara/ftpichu.c and adding a local ftpichu.h. The C implementation covers ftPichu_OnLoad, ftPichu_OnDeath, and several item/model visibility helper routines, using existing Fighter fields, held-item calls, item resource registration calls, and float constants directly in C. Build lists were updated to compile the new C object instead of the assembly object, and e_files.mk was updated for the epilogue-unscheduled object list. The only global header change was giving func_80124704 a typed HSD_GObj* prototype.
what_changed: Deleted the 319-line hand-written/disassembled assembly file asm/melee/ft/chara/ftpichu.s.
Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftpichu.c with C equivalents for ftPichu_OnLoad, ftPichu_OnDeath, func_80149FE4, func_8014A0C4, func_8014A10C, func_8014A154, func_8014A1A8, func_8014A1C8, and func_8014A20C.
Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftpichu.h with an ftPichuAttributes struct and prototypes for the newly matched Pichu functions.
Changed obj_files.mk so ftpichu is built from $(BUILD_DIR)/src/melee/ft/chara/ftpichu.o instead of $(BUILD_DIR)/asm/melee/ft/chara/ftpichu.o.
Added ftpichu.o to EPILOGUE_UNSCHEDULED in e_files.mk.
Changed include/functions.h from an unknown commented func_80124704 declaration to void func_80124704(struct _HSD_GObj*).

ftPichu_OnLoad now expresses the original setup in C: pulls Fighter from gobj->user_data, accesses ftData->x48_items, sets ft->x2224_flag.bits.b7, calls func_801243AC, reads ft->x2D4_specialAttributes, and registers three item/resource pairs through func_8026B3F8.
ftPichu_OnDeath now expresses the original x619_flag branch tree as a switch over cases 0 through 3, calling func_80074A4C with slot indices 1, 2, and 3 and values 0 or -1.

func_80149FE4 now gates behavior on func_8026B2B4(ft->x1974_heldItem) == 0, switches on func_8026B320(ft->x1974_heldItem), maps item states 1 through 4 to func_80070FB4 values 1, 0, 2, and 3, and optionally calls func_80070C48 when arg1 is nonzero.
func_8014A1C8 and func_8014A20C replaced sdata2 float labels with literal 3.0f and 0.0f arguments to func_800704F0.
smart_moves: Modeled the assembly branch ladders as C switch statements, which preserves the original case layout while making the behavior searchable and maintainable.
Introduced a minimal ftPichuAttributes struct with padding up to known offsets x14, x18, and xDC rather than overclaiming unknown fields.

Used established Fighter field names such as x10C_ftData, x48_items, x2224_flag.bits.b7, x2D4_specialAttributes, x619_flag, and x1974_heldItem to keep the implementation aligned with existing decomp conventions.
Kept unknown helper functions under their address-based names where no semantic names were evidenced, avoiding speculative renaming.
Converted small assembly float constants from sdata2 labels into clear C literals in the functions that set values to 3.0f and 0.0f.
Updated both the object list and epilogue-unscheduled list, which is necessary for replacing an asm object with a newly matched C object in this build system.
decomp_lessons: For fighter character files, matching can often proceed by creating a character-local header with only the attributes offsets needed by the current functions; unknown ranges can stay as explicit padding.
When translating branch trees that compare a byte flag against small integer states, a switch can match while clarifying repeated calls and case-specific argument changes.
Do not rename unknown item/model helper functions without evidence; address-style names such as func_80070FB4 and func_8026B3F8 remain acceptable in matched C.
Wrapper functions can be matched directly when they simply forward the HSD_GObj* to another helper, as with func_8014A1A8 calling func_80124704.
Replacing an asm file requires coordinated build-file edits: remove the asm object from TEXT_O_FILES, add the src object, and update any epilogue object lists used by the project.
Adding one typed prototype in include/functions.h can unblock C compilation without broad header churn.
naming_conventions: Character lifecycle functions use the ftCharacter_OnLoad and ftCharacter_OnDeath style, here ftPichu_OnLoad and ftPichu_OnDeath.
Character-local attribute structs use a character-specific typedef name, here ftPichuAttributes.
Unknown or not-yet-semantically-named functions keep their address-derived func_XXXXXXXX names.
Unknown struct members retain offset-based xNN or xNNN names and padding fields, such as x14, x18, xDC, x0_padding, and x18_padding.
HSD_GObj* parameters are named gobj, Fighter* locals are named ft, ftData* locals are named ftdata, and item table locals are named items in the added C.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Preserved stack/register-sensitive behavior by adding unused locals in ftPichu_OnLoad and func_80149FE4, including u32 unused[2] and s32 result, switched_result, unused.
Translated rlwimi/stb bit manipulation at Fighter offset 0x2224 into ft->x2224_flag.bits.b7 = 1.
Translated byte load from offset 0x619 into a u8 temp_r0 local and switch, matching the death-state dispatch pattern.
Mapped the held-item state dispatch from func_8026B320 into switch cases 1, 2, 3, and 4 with no default side effect, matching the assembly's fall-through to the end label for other values.
Replaced sdata2 labels lbl_804D9DB8 and lbl_804D9DBC with float literals 3.0f and 0.0f, respectively.
Used direct array indexing items[0], items[1], and items[2] to match loads from offsets 0, 4, and 8 of the item table.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that rename func_80149FE4 or other ftpichu helper functions to semantic names.
Search for other fighter character files that define similar character attribute structs with sparse padding and item resource offsets.
Compare Pikachu/Pichu OnLoad implementations for shared item registration patterns involving func_8026B3F8.
Investigate later header cleanup for func_80124704 and the func_80070FB4/func_80070C48/func_80070CC4 helper family.
